QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_9

question.text: In the equity segment, what is the STT rate for delivery-based purchase of equity shares?

answer.text: 0.25% payable by both buyer and seller

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For delivery-based purchase of equity shares, STT is 0.1% (0.100%) payable by the purchaser. For delivery-based sale, it's also 0.1% payable by the seller. So both buyer and seller pay 0.1% each on delivery trades. **The Trap:** For non-delivery (intraday) trades, only the seller pays STT at the lower rate of 0.025%. Students must distinguish delivery vs. non-delivery STT rates. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers STT on derivative transactions. Series V-A covers STT on mutual fund units. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** STT summary: Delivery equity = 0.1% (both buyer & seller). Non-delivery equity = 0.025% (seller only). Futures = 0.02% (seller). Options sale = 0.1% on premium (seller). Options exercise = 0.125% on intrinsic value (buyer). NO STT on currency/interest rate derivatives or debt.
